title: UC Davis Arboretum Waterway Trail
description: A 3.5-mile loop through 100 acres of themed gardens alongside Putah Creek. Shaded, flat, and perfectly suited for a morning jog, solo walk, or botany class. Over 4,000 plant species line the path. Open 24 hours.

title: Putah Creek Riparian Reserve Trail
address: Putah Creek Riparian Reserve, Davis, CA 95616
description: Peaceful riparian trail tracing the south bank of Putah Creek along the western edge of UC Davis. Prime birdwatching territory — over 200 species recorded here including egrets, herons, and raptors. Unpaved, shaded, and quiet.

title: Stebbins Cold Canyon Reserve
description: The most rewarding day hike near Davis. A 3.4-mile loop climbs through oak woodland and chaparral to a ridgeline with sweeping views of Lake Berryessa and the Coast Range. Wildflowers peak in March–April. Allow extra time to take it all in.

title: Cache Creek Canyon Regional Park
description: Wild oak-covered canyon 40 minutes north of Davis. The Blue Ridge Trail traverses rolling hills above Cache Creek with swimming holes at the bottom. Excellent spring wildflowers and total solitude on weekdays.

title: Lake Solano County Park
description: A beautiful stretch of Putah Creek in a narrow canyon 25 minutes from Davis. Popular for swimming, picnicking, and camping. The spring-fed creek stays cool all summer — one of the region's best hot-weather escapes.

title: American River Parkway (Jedediah Smith Trail)
address: Jedediah Smith Memorial Bicycle Trail, Sacramento, CA 95815
description: One of the finest urban trails in California — 32 miles of paved trail from Discovery Park to Folsom along the American River. Flat, wide, and accessible year-round. Perfect for a long bike ride or leisurely stroll with river views the entire way.

title: Davis Bike Loop
description: Davis is one of the most bikeable cities in America — over 100 miles of dedicated bike lanes and paths lace the city and campus. The outer loop circles the city through farm fields and neighborhoods. Flat, well-maintained, and car-free in many sections.

title: Yolo Bypass Wildlife Area
description: 16,000-acre wetlands refuge just east of Davis, managed as critical migratory bird habitat. Viewing platforms overlook flooded rice fields teeming with shorebirds, ducks, geese, and wintering sandhill cranes November through March. Free to visit.

title: Davis Wetlands
description: Managed wetlands south of Davis used for water reclamation and wildlife habitat. Elevated levee paths offer unobstructed views of shorebirds, egrets, and seasonal waterfowl. A local secret for birders and quiet walkers.

title: Mace Ranch Open Space
address: Mace Blvd & Chiles Rd, Davis, CA 95618
description: Expansive native grassland corridor on the eastern edge of Davis. Flat walking paths through oak savanna habitat — the kind of wide-open sky views that are rare this close to town. Popular with dog walkers and cyclists.

title: Tule Pond
address: Tule Pond, UC Davis, Davis, CA 95616
description: A small wildlife pond on the western edge of UC Davis campus, ringed by tule marsh and mature willows. Ducks, coots, red-winged blackbirds, and seasonal migrant warblers are regulars. Perfect for a quiet 20-minute nature break between classes.

title: Central Park Davis
description: The heart of downtown Davis — shaded by mature oaks with a splash pad, picnic tables, and an amphitheater. Home to the beloved Saturday and Wednesday farmers markets. Perfect for lazy afternoons, frisbee, or catching a free community event.

title: Community Park
description: Davis's largest park with something for everyone — disc golf course, sports fields, picnic areas, playground, basketball courts, and an off-leash dog area. Never crowded on weekday mornings.

title: Slide Hill Park
description: Davis's most beloved neighborhood park — famous for its natural grass slide down a gentle hill under a canopy of mature oaks. A genuine local icon. Come in the evening to catch the golden light filtering through the trees.

title: UC Davis Equestrian Center Trails
description: Flat unpaved paths around the campus equestrian facility on the western edge of UC Davis. Horses are often visible in adjacent paddocks. A surprisingly peaceful corner of campus with views across open farmland.

title: Discovery Park Sacramento
description: Large riverside park at the confluence of the American and Sacramento rivers. Flat shoreline walks, fishing, picnicking, and some of the most stunning river-sunset views in the region. Launch point for the American River Parkway trail.

title: Sutter's Landing Regional Park
description: Riverfront park with boat launch, kayak rentals, and stunning Sacramento River views. The paved riverside path is ideal for a long run or relaxed bike. Watch for herons and osprey fishing along the bank.

title: William Land Regional Park
address: 3800 W Land Park Dr, Sacramento, CA 95818
description: A sprawling Sacramento park with oak-shaded picnic meadows, a zoo, golf course, and WPA-era architecture. The rose garden and Japanese garden sections are especially peaceful on weekend mornings.

title: Northstar Drive Open Space
description: A natural open space corridor on Davis's northeast edge with flat walking paths through native grasses. Unobstructed panoramic views of the Sacramento Valley and the Sierra Nevada on clear winter days.

title: Woodland Prairie Park
description: A quiet Woodland community park with mature trees, picnic shelters, a small pond, and walking paths. Low-key and rarely crowded — a good option for a peaceful afternoon away from campus.

title: Sacramento State Recreation Area – Lower Sunrise
description: Beautiful stretch of the American River with sandy beaches, swimming holes, and connected trails. Popular in summer for tubing and river swimming. The Lower Sunrise trail connects to the full Jedediah Smith Parkway system.

title: Davis Central Park – Farmers Market
description: Year-round outdoor farmers market every Saturday (8am–1pm) and Wednesday (2–6pm). One of the best in California — fresh local produce, flowers, food trucks, and artisan goods. An unmissable Davis tradition.

title: Farmers Market – Woodland Downtown
description: Thursday evening farmers market in historic downtown Woodland from May through October. Fresh produce, honey, bread, and artisan crafts in a relaxed small-town setting.
